justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Distributed tracing gap explicitly identified as cross-service diagnosis blocker ('Cannot correlate a failed transition to the upstream request', 'Blast radius is invisible'). (2) 30-second log delay called out as incident response concern ('sees stale data when they need real-time', 'logs are stale during the critical first minutes'). (3) Stale runbook flagged ('18 months stale', 'actively harmful', rated P0). (4) No recovery tooling for stuck orders identified ('No event log, no outbox, no replay capability', 'Stuck orders require manual intervention'). (5) Multiple specific recommendations provided (tracing, runbook update, recovery mechanism, stuck-order gauge, failure-reason labels). (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: The 3am scenario walkthrough is exceptional — it translates abstract gaps into concrete on-call experience, showing exactly how each missing capability compounds during an incident
Correctly identified all four incident response gaps and prioritized them with effort/impact ratings
Went beyond the rubric by identifying additional gaps: slow-bleed alert threshold, poison message handling, circuit breaker absence, and the fact that rollback does not fix stuck orders
Recommendations are actionable and tiered (P0 must-haves vs nice-to-haves), with the recovery mechanism correctly rated as the highest-severity issue
Correctly noted that PagerDuty alerting is adequate while everything downstream of detection is broken
Bottom line summary crystallizes the three questions every incident requires — diagnosis, scoping, recovery — and maps each to the specific gap
